findings.title: Filtering to `Regular | InlineReply` drops valid thread opener messages
findings.description: This early return ignores every non-`Regular` message type, including Discord's `ThreadStarterMessage` (type 21). In public threads created from an existing parent message, the first message inside the thread is the synthetic thread-starter message, so this change causes the bot to skip the thread's opening prompt entirely. That is a behavioral regression specific to Discord threads, and it is easy to miss because the comment only mentions rename/pin system messages while the code filters much more broadly.
findings.suggestion: Narrow the filter to the concrete system message kinds you actually want to suppress (for example `PinsAdd`, `ThreadCreated`, channel-name/icon changes, join/boost notices, etc.), or explicitly allow `MessageType::ThreadStarterMessage` alongside `Regular` and `InlineReply` if thread-opening messages should still be processed.

summary: The diff introduces one Discord-specific behavioral regression: it suppresses `ThreadStarterMessage`, so some valid thread-opening messages will never reach the bot.
